title: Cell lysis, detergent-free
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7og5s","text":"Detergents are generally not compatible with mass spectrometers, so this is a detergent-free method of cell lysis that is compatible with mass spectrometry. Since this protocol does not have a precipitation step, it saves time and minimizes sample loss as well.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"12q88","text":"Mix well after each step by vortex for 2 seconds.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fresh or previously frozen, store on ice. Original protocol uses 5x10 7 cells per sample.

1:1 water-TFE acts as a hypotonic aqueous buffer to lyse cells, eliminating the need for detergent. TFE helps protein solubility and denaturation; it readily evaporates, so removing it is easy. undetermined, Trifluoroethanol TFE is located in NCE 435 - flammable cabinet TFE evaporates fast, so work quickly. This step should be done in the fume hood.

Original protcol calls for 2 mins at 20% duty cycle, output control 3 using a ultrasonic probe. The time required to do this for large number of samples is not realistic, and therefore not used here. 00:02:00, in water bath benchtop sonicator While this step is happening, turn on the heat block and set it at 95oC
Add TCEP (tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine to a final concentration of 10mM TCEP is located at NCE 436 -20C freezer door
Add 2-chloroacetamide to a final concentration of 40mM 2-chloroacetamide is located in NCE 438 - chemical shelves 00:10:00, 95oC in a heat block
Spindown debris at top speed on a benchtop centrifuge for 10 minutes, then keep the supernatant.
Add 1ug LysC to every 50ug protein 02:00:00 Undetermined, incubator (not heat block)
Add 1ug trypsin to every 50 ug protein. Allow reaction to occur overnight. 16:00:00, minimum reaction time Undetermined, incubator (not heat block)

Use the Coomassie reagent to measure protein concentration. Aliquot the desired amount to proceed with the remainder of this experiment. Freezing unused protein is not recommended since there is a high chance of precipitation upon thawing.
creator: Queenie Chan
